description.en: Some curses routines that return an integer, such as napms(), return OK upon success.

info.excerpt: Some curses routines that return an integer, such as napms(), return OK upon success.

curses.textpad/window/overlay.name: overlay
signatures: window.overlay(destwin[, sminrow, smincol, dminrow, dmincol, dmaxrow, dmaxcol])

parameters.name: destwin[, sminrow, smincol, dminrow, dmincol, dmaxrow, dmaxcol

description.en: Overlay the window on top of destwin. The windows need not be the same size, only the overlapping region is copied. This copy is non-destructive, which means that the current background character does not overwrite the old contents of destwin. To get fine-grained control over the copied region, the second form of overlay() can be used. sminrow and smincol are the upper-left coordinates of the source window, and the other variables mark a rectangle in the destination window.

curses.textpad/window/overwrite.name: overwrite
signatures: window.overwrite(destwin[, sminrow, smincol, dminrow, dmincol, dmaxrow, dmaxcol])

parameters.name: destwin[, sminrow, smincol, dminrow, dmincol, dmaxrow, dmaxcol

description.en: Overwrite the window on top of destwin. The windows need not be the same size, in which case only the overlapping region is copied. This copy is destructive, which means that the current background character overwrites the old contents of destwin. To get fine-grained control over the copied region, the second form of overwrite() can be used. sminrow and smincol are the upper-left coordinates of the source window, the other variables mark a rectangle in the destination window.
